bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with all valueF (worst values for me), which gives me very low utility (~0.20 across all issues). Their aggressive message is cheap talk. Since we're at t=0.00, I should open with my best bid (all valueA) to maximize my utility and anchor the negotiation high. My utility would be 1.0 across all issues = 1.0 total. I'll concede later as time progresses.

We're very early (t=0.02) and the opponent has made two identical extreme offers (all valueF) despite their aggressive messaging. Their behavior shows no concession whatsoever, so I should stay close to my top utility while making a small gesture. I'll concede slightly on my least important issue (issueD) by offering valueB instead of valueA, keeping strong positions on issueA and issueB which have the highest weights for me.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.04). I'll keep issueA at valueA (highest weight 0.4) and make modest concessions on lower-weight issues.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.06). The opponent is holding firm at their best values (valueF for everything, with minor movement on issueA to valueE). We're at t=0.10, still very early.
